item.title: The golden age of arbitrage has begun
item.description: The law of one price is in retreat — with major consequences for profits, inflation and innovation

item.title: Big Four’s shrinkage at the top presages weakness at the bottom line
item.description: Professional service firms appear to be braced for flatlining growth

item.title: Mandelson row is a cold shower for civil service relations
item.description: The fallout from an ambassadorial appointment like no other threatens even worse procedural ‘sludge’ in Whitehall

item.title: Why do I have so much in UK equities?
item.description: British pension schemes will soon be forced to support my portfolio

item.title: Is my SUV large enough?
item.description: Too large. Too ugly. Too dangerous. But can I really afford not to go big?

item.title: The dollar swap puzzle
item.description: Plus, where’s the love for clean energy?

item.title: The danger of weaponising dollar swap lines
item.description: Putative Fed chair Kevin Warsh could embrace Treasury secretary Scott Bessent’s geoeconomic agenda

item.title: Swatch’s governance under watch by investors
item.description: Fresh push by shareholder activist raises debate over board

item.title: China’s own Mythos is coming — America must prepare
item.description: The US needs to block Chinese access to the most advanced technology

item.title: Brilliant plants for balconies — and other small spaces
item.description: In pots and on patios, alpines including sweet and hardy dionysias, androsaces and saxifrages enliven even the most petite gardenscape

item.title: AI brings Foxconn a chance to cut its reliance on Apple
item.description: The cloud and networking division, which assembles AI servers, is growing at a pace the smartphone market cannot match

item.title: The welcome return of German military might
item.description: A defence strategy sets the right direction for dealing with the Russian threat

item.title: South Africa’s new Trump whisperer
item.description: Sending a white Afrikaner as ambassador to the US is a diplomatic masterstroke

item.title: Tesco and Sainsbury’s wisely stock up on market share
item.description: Both supermarkets want to avoid giving back any of their hard-won ground

item.title: How worried should Wall St be about the stablecoin threat to deposits?
item.description: The risk to US banks is real in theory but difficult to realise in practice

item.title: Labour is being destroyed by its former saviours
item.description: Factionalism effectively quashed the Corbynites but has undermined Keir Starmer’s leadership of party and country

item.title: The politics of terror
item.description: Putin and Trump are among the powerful individuals radicalising into violence

item.title: Britons are now working like Americans
item.description: Hours worked per person across the two countries have converged, but the productivity gap has widened

item.title: Europe’s resistance to Chinese investment has stalled
item.description: The European Commission’s repeated attempts to run a coherent green industrial strategy are showing few results

item.title: A vibe check from commodities pros
item.description: Plus UK inflation

item.title: The secret diary of a middle power
item.description: So much economic conflict, so many feelings

item.title: It’s a ‘sontaku’ world — we are just living in it
item.description: Pre-emptive interpretation of a leader’s wishes can be dangerous and reveals a vacuum where direction should be

item.title: War still weighs on stocks despite the S&P 500’s rebound
item.description: The fact that higher growth expectations have not led to higher valuations suggests that investors are more wary of what the future holds

item.title: That sweater isn’t fooling anyone, big guy
item.description: Presidents and executives have tried to pull the wool over people’s eyes and soften their image with knitwear

item.title: SpaceX and Tesla are on an inevitable collision course
item.description: To the extent shareholders just want exposure to Elon Musk, a single security seems like the sensible end state
